title: Seeded Recursive Backtracking Generation · Behavior Case: Recursive Iterative Tradeoff
summary: Generation chooses visited neighbors, recursive depth overflows silently, random state is global, and completion is confused with connectivity. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the recursive iterative tradeoff increment in seeded iterative and recursive backtracker. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare recursive and explicit-stack backtracking for clarity, depth, pause, serialization, and failure behavior.
Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-generation-backtracker-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-generation-backtracker-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_generation_backtracker_5(cells: int = 48, visited: int = 48, carved_edges: int = 47, stack_peak: int = 21, replay_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0:
        return False, visited, "generation-count"
    if visited != cells:
        return False, visited, "unvisited-cell"
    if carved_edges != cells - 1:
        return False, carved_edges, "tree-shape"
    if stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match:
        return False, stack_peak, "replay-invariant"
    assert visited == cells and carved_edges == cells - 1
    return True, visited, "seeded-backtracker"

For repair the recursive iterative tradeoff defect, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Recursive Iterative Tradeoff, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recursive Iterative Tradeoff model · Theory Recursive…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Recursive Iterative Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 8688 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare recursive and explicit-stack backtracking for clarity, depth, pause, serialization, and failure behavior.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recursive Iterative Tradeoff model · Theory Recursive….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recursive Iterative Tradeoff model · Theory Recursive…, keep Recursive Iterative Tradeoff connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare recursive and explicit-stack backtracking for clarity, depth, pause, serialization, and failure behavior.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recursive Iterative Tradeoff model · Theory Recursive…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Recursive Iterative Tradeoff precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recursive Iterative Tradeoff model · Theory Recursive…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 8688 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare recursive and explicit-stack backtracking for clarity, depth, pause, serialization, and failure behavior.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recursive Iterative Tradeoff model · Theory Recursive…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Recursion is the algorithm while a stack would change its result.
